pattern: The k closest elements form a contiguous window in the sorted array, so binary search for the best window start instead of scoring every element.
hints: Because the array is sorted, the answer is always a run of k consecutive elements; you only need to find where that run begins.
Compare the element just before a candidate window with the element just after it to decide which side is closer to x, using the tie rule that prefers the smaller value.
Binary search the left boundary over the range of valid window starts, shrinking toward whichever side keeps the closer neighbor.

stress.description: random sorted array with random k and x
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    arr = sorted(rng.randint(-10000, 10000) for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    x = rng.randint(-10000, 10000)
    return [arr, k, x]
stress.note: covers ties, out-of-range x, and interior windows
